hypothesis:
After 12 iterations, the core problem is clear: the dual momentum family with 8 ETFs generates too few trades because rankings are sticky. The fundamental insight from iteration 9 (best attempt: IS Sharpe 0.47, OOS 0.58, 7/7 WF consistency, but only 13.875 trades) is that the concept works but needs more signal diversity.

This iteration takes a completely different code architecture to GUARANTEE 50+ trades: instead of tracking rank changes between rebalance periods, we use a CONTINUOUS SIGNAL approach where we generate individual BUY signals for EACH ETF independently when it meets criteria, and EXIT signals when it fails.

The approach:
1. For each of the 8 ETFs daily, compute: (a) 20-day momentum rank (relative), (b) 20-day return > 0 (absolute/soft filter), (c) RSI(14) < 60 as entry gate
2. ENTRY signal for each ETF when: it is in the top-3 by 20-day return AND 20-day return > 0
3. EXIT signal for each ETF when: it falls out of top-3 by 20-day return OR its 20-day return turns negative
4. Daily evaluation of signals but NO forced full-portfolio turnover (that killed edge in iter 11)

Key insight: by evaluating signals DAILY rather than on a fixed rebalance schedule, we allow natural entry/exit when rankings genuinely change. The 20-day lookback changes rankings far more frequently than 63-day (iter 9). With 8 ETFs and daily evaluation of a 20-day momentum window, rankings shuffle ~3-4x per month per ETF = ~250-400 signal events per year across all ETFs. Even if 80% are held continuously, the 20% turnover events = 50+ trades per year = 250+ over 5-year IS period.

Why 20-day lookback beats 63-day for trade count: 20-day returns are more volatile, causing more rank shuffling. The academic literature (Jegadeesh & Titman) found momentum works at 3-12 months, but the ETF rotation literature shows 20-60 day windows work for high-liquidity ETFs. 

The absolute momentum filter (return > 0) is kept as a SOFT filter — it reduces drawdown without completely suppressing trades because with only 8 ETFs and top-3 selection, if 5+ ETFs are trending up, 3 will always pass. This avoids the cash-trap issue.

Long-only, no leverage, fees=0.001.

evaluation.post_mortem: Failed transaction cost gate: post-cost IS Sharpe = -0.02. Pre-cost IS Sharpe was 0.06. The edge is too thin to survive realistic trading costs.
evaluation.next_direction: Strategy needs a larger edge. Reduce trade frequency or focus on higher-conviction signals.
